coverage_note: 11 real named floating units captured across all required sub-types: 3 FPSOs (Terra Nova, SeaRose, Noble Seillean), 1 FDPSO (Azurite), 3 drillships (Yavuz, Fatih, Deepwater Conqueror), 1 production semisubmersible (Thunder Horse PDQ), 2 spars (Perdido, Genesis), 1 FSRU (Independence). LAYER-1 principal particulars (LOA, beam, often depth + one draft + displacement/tonnage) are well-sourced from public Wikipedia infoboxes and one OEM brochure. CRITICAL DATA-AVAILABILITY REALITY: (1) LBP is essentially never published in public sources for these units — universally a gap, which in turn blocks computed block coefficient. (2) Transit/tow draft published only for Deepwater Conqueror. (3) LAYER-2 mass properties — LCG, VCG, TCG, and radii of gyration kxx/kyy/kzz — are NOT publicly published for any of these vessels; these are proprietary class/loading-manual data behind operator NDAs. Every mass-property and radius-of-gyration field is therefore either 'gap' or 'estimated:<relation>' with the empirical relation named (ship-shaped: kxx=0.34*beam, kyy=kzz≈0.25-0.26*LOA since LBP unavailable; drillships use kxx=0.40*beam to reflect derrick-raised VCG; semisub uses column-stabilized 0.30-0.40*footprint rule; spar uses slender-cylinder kxx≈0.27*draft and small kzz≈0.5*radius). NO bare invented numbers were used. SOURCE-QUALITY CAVEATS: Deepwater Conqueror figures come from a brochure PDF whose binary was not directly parseable, so values are relayed via the search engine's read of that PDF (flagged brochure-pdf). Perdido and Genesis spar dimensions (height/diameter/draft) come from secondary trade-press summaries (Offshore Magazine/OGJ) rather than a primary datasheet; their Wikipedia pages give only weights/water-depth. FDPSO Azurite's displacement is unit-ambiguous in the source (long tons vs short tons conflict) and is flagged. Block coefficient is marked n/a for spars and semisubs (not monohulls). Radii-of-gyration relations are standard offshore-engineering rules of thumb for first-pass hydrodynamic modelling and must be replaced with class-approved loading-manual values for any design-grade RAO/mooring analysis.

verdict.summary: Spot-checked 5 of 8 records via web search (Terra Nova, SeaRose, Azurite, Yavuz, Thunder Horse PDQ, plus Deepwater Conqueror partial). All HARD dimensions match their cited sources exactly: Terra Nova 292.2x45.5 m / 193,000 t; SeaRose 258x46 m / 190,102 t / 18.043 m draught; Azurite 322.22x56 m / 19.667 m; Yavuz 229.19x36 m / 14.7 m / 51,283 GT; Thunder Horse 136x112x30 m / 130,000 t / 59,500 t. Cited Wikipedia/brochure URLs are real and on-topic. No invented hard numbers dressed as facts. Every estimated value carries an explicit 'estimated:' prefix with a stated formula; every missing value is marked 'gap'. Radii-of-gyration estimates use defensible, transparent conventions (kxx=0.34*B tanker rule; 0.40*B elevated for derrick-heavy drillships; column-stabilized rule for the semisub instead of the ship rule), and the LBP-missing -> LOA-proxy substitution is disclosed (slightly overstates kyy/kzz, but flagged). Methodology is honest and conservative. Only nits: (1) Azurite displacement_t shows raw '291199' which is short tons not tonnes, though the embedded note correctly reconciles to ~264,000 t (259,999 long tons = 264,177 t = 291,199 short tons, internally consistent); (2) Terra Nova IMO (actually 9183532) and SeaRose GT/NT are publicly available but conservatively left as gap/omitted -- under-claiming, not fabrication.

flagged_records.name: FDPSO Azurite
flagged_records.issue: displacement_t field literal value '291199' is in SHORT TONS, not tonnes; correct metric displacement is ~264,177 t. The embedded note flags the ambiguity and reconciles correctly, but the headline number is unit-mislabeled and could mislead a downstream consumer reading only the field value.

flagged_records.name: Deepwater Conqueror (drillship)
flagged_records.issue: Only brochure-PDF source (binary, not directly parsed); hull dimensions could not be independently re-confirmed via search excerpts (URL is real and live). year_built correctly left as gap (actual: 2016, IMO 9730804). Thinnest citation of the set but not fabricated.

flagged_records.name: Terra Nova FPSO
flagged_records.issue: IMO listed as gap but is publicly known (9183532); under-claim only, no integrity issue.

records.name: Terra Nova FPSO
records.vessel_id: Builder: Daewoo Shipbuilding & Marine Engineering, Okpo, South Korea

fields.block_coefficient: estimated:Cb=disp/(rho*LBP*B*T); cannot compute without LBP and operating draft -> gap until those published
fields.LCG_m: estimated:typical ship-shaped FPSO LCG near midships, ~0.48-0.50*LBP from AP; no published value -> gap
fields.VCG_m: estimated:VCG~0.40-0.50*depth at operating/storage condition; no published value -> gap
fields.kyy_pitch_m: estimated:kyy=0.25*LOA=0.25*292.2=73.1 (LBP not published; LOA used as proxy)
fields.kzz_yaw_m: estimated:kzz=0.26*LOA=0.26*292.2=76.0 (LBP not published; LOA used as proxy)
fields.loading_condition: displacement quoted is full/operating; specific condition not stated

fields.block_coefficient: estimated:Cb=disp/(rho*LBP*B*T); LBP not published -> gap (VLCC hulls typ. Cb~0.80-0.84)

records.name: Thunder Horse PDQ
records.vessel_id: Builder: Daewoo Shipbuilding & Marine Engineering, Okpo; water depth 1,840 m, Gulf of Mexico

fields.block_coefficient: n/a — semisubmersible (column-stabilized, no monohull block coefficient)
fields.LCG_m: estimated:near geometric centre of square column footprint by symmetry; no published value -> gap
fields.VCG_m: estimated:semisub VCG typically near or just below operating waterline / deck base; no published value -> gap
fields.kxx_roll_m: estimated:semisub kxx~kyy~0.30-0.40*column-spacing; with ~78 m diagonal footprint kxx~0.35*112=39 (column-stabilized rule, NOT the 0.34*beam ship rule)
fields.loading_condition: operating/survival draft 30 m, full displacement 130,000 t

gaps: radii of gyration (no published values — semisub-specific estimates only)

records.name: Perdido spar
records.vessel_id: Hull builder: Technip, Pori, Finland; Topsides: Kiewit Offshore Services, Corpus Christi; water depth 2,450 m, Gulf of Mexico

fields.overall_height_m: 169 (555 ft) — figure from web search summary; not on Wikipedia page
fields.hull_diameter_m: 36 (118 ft, hard tank) — from web search summary; not on Wikipedia page
fields.draft_operating_m: 154 (505 ft) — from web search summary; not on Wikipedia page
fields.displacement_t: gap (Wikipedia gives spar hull weight 22,000 t and topsides 9,500 t, which are weights not buoyant displacement)
fields.block_coefficient: n/a — spar (vertical cylinder, no block coefficient)
fields.LCG_m: estimated:on vertical centreline by axial symmetry; horizontal LCG~0 relative to hull axis
fields.VCG_m: estimated:spar designed for deep VCG below centre of buoyancy for stability; specific value not published -> gap
fields.kxx_roll_m: estimated:spar pitch/roll radius dominated by deep draft; kxx~kyy~0.25-0.30*draft~0.27*154=42 (slender-cylinder estimate)
fields.kzz_yaw_m: estimated:kzz~0.5*hull_radius~0.5*18=9 (small; yaw inertia of slender cylinder dominated by radius)

gaps: radii of gyration (estimates only; no published values)
height/diameter/draft are from secondary web summaries, not the primary Wikipedia infobox

citations.source: Web search summary citing Offshore Magazine / OGJ / Green Car Congress coverage of Perdido spar (505 ft draft, 555 ft height, 118 ft hard-tank diameter)

records.name: FSRU Independence

fields.block_coefficient: estimated:LNG-carrier hull Cb~0.75-0.80; cannot compute (disp & LBP missing) -> gap
